content.text:
```mdl
@ Non‑Disclosure Agreement for the Exchange of Energy Management System Model Data
module contract_nli_no_align_dev_13_nda_13

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Types
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Clause 1 – Parties
type Party = {
    name: string,
    is_pjm: bool                # true for PJM, false for the Transmission Owner
}

@ Clause 2 – Reviewing Representative
type ReviewingRepresentative = {
    name: string,
    title: string,
    party: Party,
    has_certificate: bool,      # true when a signed Non‑Disclosure Certificate has been delivered
    has_competitive_duties: bool
}

@ Clause 4 – Confidential Information
type ConfidentialInformation = {
    description: string,
    is_marked_confidential: bool,
    source_party: Party
}

@ Clause 7 – Third‑Party Request
type ThirdPartyRequest = {
    requestor: string,
    reason: string
}

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Entities
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Clause 1 – each Party (PJM and the Transmission Owner)
entity party: Party

@ Clause 2 – each Reviewing Representative who signs a certificate
entity reviewing_rep: ReviewingRepresentative

@ Clause 4 – a piece of Confidential Information exchanged under the Agreement
entity conf_info: ConfidentialInformation

@ Clause 7 – a request from a third party for disclosure
entity third_party_req: ThirdPartyRequest

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper Functions
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Clause 2 – a Reviewing Representative must have a signed certificate before
#           accessing Confidential Information.
func can_access_confidential(rep: ReviewingRepresentative) -> bool:
    rep.has_certificate

@ Clause 5 – a Party may disclose Confidential Information to a third party
#           only if it has obtained prior written approval from the Disclosing Party.
func has_written_approval(conf: ConfidentialInformation) -> bool:
    -- In a real model this would be linked to an approval record.
    -- For the purpose of the logical model we assume no approval by default.
    false

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Rules (Deontic constraints)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Clause 2 – Obligation to deliver a signed Non‑Disclosure Certificate
rule O deliver_certificate:
    reviewing_rep.has_certificate always

@ Clause 2 – Obligation: only a Reviewing Representative with a certificate
#           may access Confidential Information.
rule O only_certified_rep_may_access:
    (conf_info.is_marked_confidential implies can_access_confidential(reviewing_rep)) always

@ Clause 5 – Prohibition: disclose Confidential Information to a third party
#           without prior written approval.
rule F disclose_confidential_to_third_party_without_approval:
    (conf_info.is_marked_confidential and not has_written_approval(conf_info)) always

@ Clause 5 – Obligation: Parties shall not disclose Confidential Information
#           to any third party without prior written approval.
rule O no_unauthorized_third_party_disclosure:
    (conf_info.is_marked_confidential and not has_written_approval(conf_info)) always

@ Clause 8 – Obligation: return or destroy Confidential Information when it
#           is no longer needed for Transmission Functions.
rule O return_or_destroy_when_not_needed:
    -- The model does not track the “need” state; we express the obligation
    -- as a generic requirement that the information must eventually be
    -- returned or destroyed.
    (conf_info.is_marked_confidential) eventually

@ Clause 9 – Permission: acquire information that is *not* marked confidential
#           from a third party (i.e., “similar” information that is not confidential).
rule P acquire_nonconfidential_info_from_third_party:
    (not conf_info.is_marked_confidential) always

@ Clause 9 – Prohibition: acquire confidential information from a third party
#           without approval (reinforces the prohibition above).
rule F acquire_confidential_info_from_third_party_without_approval:
    (conf_info.is_marked_confidential and not has_written_approval(conf_info)) always

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Hypothesis (for verification)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ The hypothesis to be checked against the model:
#   “Receiving Party may acquire information similar to Confidential Information
#    from a third party.”
#   This is expressed by the permission rule above (acquire_nonconfidential_info_from_third_party).
#   If a verifier finds a conflict with rule F acquire_confidential_info_from_third_party_without_approval,
#   the hypothesis would be false for confidential material.
```
